questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which Indian author won the 2025 International Booker Prize for the short story collection 'Heart Lamp'?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. Banu Mushtaq won the 2025 International Booker Prize for 'Heart Lamp'.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which of the following quantities remains unchanged when a charged particle moves in a magnetic field?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. Kinetic energy remains unchanged when a charged particle moves in a magnetic field.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which of the following is responsible for managing shortcut links and desktop visual layout?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Windows Shell manages shortcut links and desktop visual layout.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: India has unveiled a comprehensive plan worth ₹9.15 lakh crore (US$109.50 billion) to enhance its power infrastructure and meet a projected demand of 458 GW by
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. India plans to meet a projected demand of 458 GW by 2032.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Under the Government of India Act, 1919, which subjects were transferred to Indian ministers?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. Education and Health were transferred to Indian ministers under the Act of 1919.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: The formation of meanders in rivers is primarily caused by which of the following?
options: Sudden uplift of riverbeds by tectonic activity
Lateral erosion on the outer banks and deposition on the inner banks
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. Meanders form due to lateral erosion on outer banks and deposition on inner banks.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which neighbouring country did India accuse of supporting cross-border infiltration attempts in Jammu and Kashmir in early 2025?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. India accused Pakistan of supporting cross-border infiltration attempts.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which of the following countries will restrict the Mt. Everest Permit to only those who have already climbed 7000 m?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. Nepal will restrict Mt. Everest permits to those who have climbed 7000 m.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: The Contact Process is used for the manufacturing of which of the following chemicals?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. The Contact Process is used to manufacture Sulphuric acid.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: During the rule of which of the following Mughal rulers did Sunnis and Shias meet in one mosque and Christians and Jews in one church to pray, thus following the principle of 'universal peace' (sulh-i kul)?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. Akbar followed the principle of sulh-i kul, promoting religious tolerance.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: In which of the following Indian states does the Jagar dance form have its primary roots?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Jagar dance has its primary roots in Himachal Pradesh.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: The Third Battle of Panipat in 1761 was fought between Ahmad Shah Abdali and which Indian power?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. The Third Battle of Panipat was fought between Ahmad Shah Abdali and the Marathas.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: As per Census 2011, which of the following is the most populous city in India?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. Mumbai is the most populous city in India as per Census 2011.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which policy initiative aims to digitise all Primary Agricultural Credit Societies (PACS)?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. The PACS Digitization Project aims to digitise all Primary Agricultural Credit Societies.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Pamban Port is located in which state of India?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Pamban Port is located in Tamil Nadu.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: What does the shortcut key Ctrl + S do in most software applications?
options: Searches for text in a document
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Ctrl + S saves the current document in most applications.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.subTopic: Elections and Representation of People Act
questions.question: Who was the first Chief Election Commissioner of India?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Sukumar Sen was the first Chief Election Commissioner of India.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Where was the 2025 Regional Forum on Sustainable Development (RFSD) for the UNECE region held on April 2-3?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. The 2025 RFSD for the UNECE region was held in Geneva.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Where was the BrahMos Aerospace Integration and Testing Facility inaugurated on 11 May 2025?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The BrahMos facility was inaugurated in Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Who translated the book 'Heart Lamp' into English, making it the first Kannada work to win the 2025 International Booker Prize?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Deepa Bhashthi translated 'Heart Lamp' into English.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which of the following tribal uprisings was led by Siddhu and Kanhu Murmu?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The Santhal rebellion was led by Siddhu and Kanhu Murmu.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which of the following is considered the first full-length Indian feature film, released in 1913?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. 'Raja Harishchandra' is considered the first full-length Indian feature film.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which Indian state had the highest Human Development Index (HDI) value according to the NITI Aayog's SDG India Index 2023?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. Kerala had the highest HDI value according to the SDG India Index 2023.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which DRDO-developed system, inducted into the Indian Army in 2024, played a crucial role in air defence during Operation Sindoor in 2025?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The Akash system played a crucial role in air defence during Operation Sindoor.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: What evidence supports the layered structure of the Earth's interior?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. Seismic wave behaviour supports the layered structure of Earth's interior.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which of the following was a major drawback of the Indian economy at the time of independence?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. High unemployment and poverty were major drawbacks at the time of independence.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Who is the director of the Indian film 'Tanvi: The Great', which had its world premiere at the Cannes Film Market during the 78th Cannes Film Festival in 2025?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Anupam Kher is the director of 'Tanvi: The Great'.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which of the following diseases is caused by protozoa?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. Malaria is caused by protozoa.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: The early phase of the Indian National Congress (1885-1905) was characterised by which approach?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The early Congress used the approach of petition and prayer.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which term refers to the pre-monsoon showers that occur with a sudden rise in temperature, especially in southern India?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. Mango showers are pre-monsoon showers in southern India.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.subTopic: Biosphere Reserves National Parks Wildlife Sanctuaries
questions.question: Which of the following is the first biosphere reserve established in India?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Nilgiri is the first biosphere reserve established in India.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: In March 2025, the Defence Ministry signed contracts with which two companies for the procurement of 155 mm/52 calibre Advanced Towed Artillery Gun System (ATAGS)?
options: Bharat Forge Ltd and Tata Advanced Systems Ltd
Larsen & Toubro Ltd and MTAR Technologies Ltd
Mahindra Defence Systems Ltd and Kalyani Strategic Systems Ltd
Bharat Dynamics Ltd and Hindustan Aeronautics Ltd
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Contracts were signed with Bharat Forge Ltd and Tata Advanced Systems Ltd.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: In which list of the Seventh Schedule does the Central Government have exclusive power to legislate?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The Central Government has exclusive power to legislate on the Union List.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: The Aguada Fort, which is a monument to Portuguese construction and engineering, is situated in which of the following states of India?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. Aguada Fort is situated in Goa.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: The National Multidimensional Poverty Index, published by NITI Aayog, measures poverty beyond income by considering multiple deprivations. Which of the following dimensions is NOT included in India's National Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI)?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. Employment is NOT included in India's National MPI.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which of the following policy initiatives aims to promote the growth of the solar energy sector in India?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. RECs aim to promote the growth of the solar energy sector.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which Indian startup unveiled a system to run AI without advanced chips in April 2025?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Ziroh Labs unveiled a system to run AI without advanced chips.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which Schedule of the Constitution deals with the administration of Scheduled Areas and Tribes?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. The Fifth Schedule deals with the administration of Scheduled Areas and Tribes.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: What is used to measure a decent standard of living in HDI?
options: GDP per capita in PPP US Dollars
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. GDP per capita in PPP US Dollars measures a decent standard of living in HDI.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which of the following multinational air combat exercises did the Indian Air Force (IAF) participate in at Al Dhafra Air Base in the UAE from 21 April to 8 May 2025?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The IAF participated in Desert Flag-10 in the UAE.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: By selling 6 rubber-bands for a rupee, a man loses 40%. To gain 20% how many must he sell for a rupee?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. He must sell 3 rubber-bands for a rupee to gain 20 percent.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: A number, when increased by 100%, gives 2780. The number is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. The number is 1390 because 1390 plus 100 percent of 1390 equals 2780.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: The product of two consecutive natural numbers is 210. The greater of the two numbers is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The greater number is 15 because 14 multiplied by 15 equals 210.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Given that 149^0.59 = x, 149^0.94 = y and x^2 = y^z, then the value of z is close to:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. The value of z is close to 7.39 based on the given equations.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: The average of first 151 even numbers is
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. The average of first 151 even numbers is 152.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: In an election between two candidates, 80% of the registered voters cast their vote and 5% of the votes polled were found invalid. The winning candidate got 75% of the valid votes and won the election by a margin of 1805 votes. How many voters were registered?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. The number of registered voters is 4750.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Pipe A can fill a tank in 16 hours, pipe B can fill the same tank in 25 hours and pipe C can fill the same tank in 12 hours. The time taken by them to fill the same tank if they operate together is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. The time taken is 5 hours when all pipes work together.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Find the mean proportional of 25 and 16.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. The mean proportional of 25 and 16 is 20.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Ram went to a shop and observed that the list price of a fan is 3,600. If the retailer has bought the fan for 2,800, then find the trade discount (correct to one decimal place).
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. The trade discount is 22.2 percent.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: In covering a distance of 182 km, Salaj takes 4 hours more than Kevin. If Salaj doubles his speed, then he would take 9 hours less than Kevin. Salaj's speed is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Salaj's speed is 7 km/hr.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Anubhav and Gaurav start a business. Anubhav invests 55,000 more than Gaurav for 2 months and Gaurav invests for 5 months. Anubhav's share is 1,431 more than that of Gaurav, out of a total profit of 7,155. Find the capital contributed by Anubhav.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Anubhav contributed 90,000.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: The LCM of the numbers 2.7 and 0.096 is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The LCM of 2.7 and 0.096 is 21.6.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: The H.C.F. and the L.C.M. of two numbers are 2 and 492, respectively. If one of the numbers is 82, find the other one.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. The other number is 12.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Simplify the following: 2(((4/2)x^2) - 21x + 15) - 4(x^2 + 5x - 15)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. Simplifying gives -62x + 90.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: By selling an article at 2/5 of its actual selling price, Manav incurs a loss of 24%. If he sells it at 88% of its actual selling price, then the profit percentage is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. The profit percentage is 67.2 percent.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Ishaan and Meghna have to travel from Delhi to Kanpur in their respective cars. Ishaan is driving at 77 km/hr while Meghna is driving at 33 km/hr. Find the time taken by Meghna to reach Kanpur if Ishaan takes 12 hours.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Meghna takes 28 hours to reach Kanpur.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.subTopic: Part to Part and Part to Whole
questions.question: X, Y and Z invested a sum in the ratio of 86 : 60 : 24, respectively. If they earned a total profit of ₹4,590 at the end of the year, what is the difference between the shares of Y and Z?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. The difference between shares of Y and Z is 972.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Which of the following numbers is divisible by 24?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. 6830904 is divisible by 24.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: A product is sold for ₹3,400 after a 15% discount. What was the original marked price?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The original marked price was 4,000.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: If sin theta = 1/2, then 13 x cos theta - 3 x cot theta - 15 is equal to:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. The value is 68.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Naman and Omar can complete a certain piece of work in 13 and 4 days, respectively. They started to work together, and after 2 days, Omar left. In how many days will Naman complete the remaining work?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. Naman will complete the remaining work in 6.5 days.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Find the value of: 83^(-6) + 83^(16) x 83^(-8)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. The value is 83 to the power of -26.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: The salary of a person was reduced by 8%. By what percentage should the reduced salary be increased to restore the original salary?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. The reduced salary should be increased by 52.2 percent.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: An exterior angle and an interior angle of a regular polygon are in the ratio of 1 : 8. What is the number of sides of the polygon?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The polygon has 18 sides.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Find the simple interest on ₹1,200 at an interest rate of 3% per month for 12 months.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The simple interest is 432.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: The number of prime numbers lying between 303 and 311, both included, is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. There are 2 prime numbers between 303 and 311.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: The volume of a right circular cylinder is 8624 cm^3. If the radius and height of the cylinder are equal, then find the height of the cylinder. (Use pi = 22/7)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The height of the cylinder is 14 cm.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Find the simple interest on ₹4,000 at 7.5% per annum rate of interest deposited on 7 February 2024 and withdrawn on 8 April 2024.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. The simple interest is 50.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: If 3 times the mother's age is 26 years more than 5 times her daughter's age, and 2 times the daughter's age is 2 years less than the mother's age, then what is the difference (in years) between the ages of the mother and the daughter?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The difference between their ages is 23 years.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Find the volume (in cm^3) of a sphere having a diameter of 22cm, giving your answer correct to two places of decimal. Take Pi = 22/7.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. The volume of the sphere is 5577.52 cm^3.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Refer to the following number and symbol series and answer the question that follows. Counting to be done from left to right only. (Left) 96S 7O xi S 1*5433S 6@# #8% (Right) How many such symbols are there each of which is immediately preceded by a number and also immediately followed by a number?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. There is 1 symbol that meets the condition.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusion(s) logically follow(s) from the statements. Statements: Some penguins are sharks. Some sharks are dolphins. Conclusions (I): No shark is a penguin. Conclusions (II): Some penguins are dolphins.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Neither conclusion follows from the given statements.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: KGDl is related to IEBJ in a certain way based on the English alphabetical order. In the same way, NJGO is related to LHEM. To which of the given options is QMJR related, following the same logic?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. QMJR is related to OKPH following the same pattern.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: If 2 is added to each even digit and 1 is subtracted from each odd digit in the number 7364592, what will be the sum of the digits which are first from the left and first from the right in the new number thus formed?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The sum of the digits is 10.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: H, I, J, K, U, V and W are sitting around a circular table facing the centre of the table. Only four people sit between U and V when counted from the right of U. U sits third to the left of W. K sits third to the left of H. I is an immediate neighbour of both V and W. How many people sit between J and I when counted from the right of J?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. Four people sit between J and I when counted from the right of J.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Veer starts from Point Y and drives 34 km towards north. He then takes a left turn, drives 20 km. He then turns left and drives 40 km. He takes a final left turn, drives 20 km and stops at Point Z. How far (shortest distance) and towards which direction should he drive in order to reach Point Y again? (All turns are 90 degrees turns only unless specified.)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Veer should drive 6 km towards north.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series? 178 142 117 101 92 ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. The next number in the series is 88.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series? 499 500 502 505 509 ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The next number in the series is 514.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Seven boxes U, V, W, X, E, F and G are kept one over the other but not necessarily in the same order. Only X is kept above W. Only two boxes are kept between W and G. Only U is kept below F. V is not kept immediately above G. How many boxes are kept between E and U?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. Three boxes are kept between E and U.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Refer to the following number and symbol series and answer the question that follows. All numbers are single-digit numbers. Counting to be done from left to right only. (Left) #1* 56 4+7 8 2 @ % & 3 9 (Right) How many such symbols are there each of which is immediately preceded by a number and also immediately followed by another number?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. There is 1 symbol that meets the condition.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Based on the English alphabetical order, three of the following four letter-cluster pairs are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which letter-cluster pair DOES NOT belong to that group? (Note: The odd one out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter-cluster.)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. NK-PL does not belong to the group.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: In a row of people all facing north, Cherry is 25th from the right end. Dinesh is 28th from the right end. Dinesh is exactly between Cherry and Lina. If Lina is 11th from the left end of the line, how many people are there in the row?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. There are 42 people in the row.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Deepti starts from point A and walks 5 km towards west. She takes a left turn and walks 2 km. She then takes a right turn and walks 3 km. She then takes a final right turn and walks 2 km and reaches a point B. How far (shortest distance) and towards which direction should she walk in order to reach point A again? (All turns are 90 degrees turns only unless specified.)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. Deepti should walk 2 km east.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Kartik ranked 19th from the top and 27th from the bottom in his class. How many students are there in his class?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. There are 45 students in the class.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Each of the digits in the number 8357416 is arranged in ascending order from left to right. What will be the sum of the digits which are second from the left and second from the right in the new number thus formed?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. The sum of the digits is 10.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: A, B, C, D, J, K and L are sitting around a circular table, facing the centre of the table. Only four people sit between C and J when counted from the right of C. C sits third to the left of B. A sits third to the left of D. L is an immediate neighbour of both J and B. How many people sit between K and L when counted from the right of K?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Four people sit between K and L when counted from the right of K.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: In a certain code language, 'OPEN' is coded as '4627' and 'EARS' is coded as '1375'. What is the code for 'E' in the given code language?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. The code for 'E' is 1.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: In a certain code language, 'KNOW' is coded as '8901' and 'KNOT' is coded as '9028'. What is the code for 'W' in the given code language?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The code for 'W' is 1.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series? 260,310,460,710,1060,?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. The next number in the series is 1510.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: In the following number-pairs, the second number is obtained by applying certain mathematical operations to the first number. Select the pair in which the numbers are related in the same way as are the numbers of the following pairs. (Note: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g., 13 - Operations on 13 such as adding/subtracting/multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.) 24,16 48,32
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. 84 and 56 follow the same pattern.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Based on the English alphabetical order, three of the following four letter-clusters are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which letter-cluster DOES NOT belong to that group? (Note: The odd one out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter-cluster.)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. MRO does not belong to the group.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Read the given statement(s) and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statement(s) is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statement(s). Statements: All detergents are towels. All detergents are conditioners. Conclusions: (I) Some towels are conditioners. (II) All conditioners are detergents.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. Only conclusion (I) follows from the statements.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Which of the following letter-number clusters will replace the question mark (?) in the given series to make it logically complete? VPM 153, QUJ 145, LZG 137, GED 129, BJA 121, ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. WOX 113 will replace the question mark.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: What will come in place of the question mark (?) in the following equation, if '+' and 'x' are interchanged and '-' and '+' are interchanged? 11 - 3 x 88 + 8 / 10 = ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The value is 32.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: MOLK is related to RTQP in a certain way based on the English alphabetical order. In the same way, GIFE is related to LNKJ. To which of the given options is VXUT related, following the same logic?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. VXUT is related to AYZS.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: S is the mother of R. R is the brother of P. P is the wife of I. I is the father of O. How is S related to O?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. S is O's mother's mother.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: If 2 is added to each even digit and 2 is subtracted from each odd digit in the number 3659247, then what will be the sum of all the even digits in the new number thus formed?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. The sum of all even digits is 18.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: XSLE is related to CWQI in a certain way based on the English alphabetical order. In the same way, RIFU is related to WMKY. To which of the given options is GUUG related, following the same logic?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. GUUG is related to LYZK.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Based on the English alphabetical order, three of the following four letter-clusters are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which letter-cluster DOES NOT belong to that group? (Note: The odd one out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter-cluster.)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. EGB does not belong to the group.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series based on the English alphabetical order? QMS NJP KGM HDJ ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. EAG will replace the question mark.
